label: Heavy going
summary:
The Middle East still sets the tone, but with the first real de-escalation signals in weeks. On June 1 President Trump said Israel and Hezbollah had agreed to halt hostilities, with Israel pledging not to strike Beirut's southern suburbs and Hezbollah vowing to stop its attacks; high-stakes Israel-Lebanon talks opened at the US State Department and run into a second day. A tentative US-Iran understanding would extend the fragile ceasefire by 60 days and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Yet the violence has not stopped: Israeli strikes killed at least eight people in Lebanon overnight, Lebanon's health ministry counts more than 3,400 dead since March, Iran briefly suspended talks over the Lebanon and Gaza fighting, and fresh Gaza strikes drew new ceasefire-violation complaints. Sudan's war remains the day's darkest thread, with an SAF drone strike on a wedding in Kutum reported to have killed dozens.

The economy is steady rather than buoyant. Asian and US futures were flat to mixed as AI enthusiasm met lingering geopolitical and energy risk; the Nikkei slipped about 0.3% while Hong Kong's Hang Seng rose around 2.5%. Climate stays a drag without a new acute disaster today: Europe's record-shattering May heatwave and the UN's warning that a record-hot year is near-certain by 2030 still frame the season, partly offset by news that wind and solar out-generated gas worldwide in April.

There is genuine light at the edges. Platypuses are breeding again along Australia's Hacking River for the first time in generations, and physicists in China reported the first direct observation of the long-predicted Migdal effect, opening new avenues in the hunt for dark matter. The headline lands at 47 - uneasy and mixed, nudged up from yesterday by tentative ceasefire diplomacy, but held well below 'good' by continuing strikes, Sudan's atrocities and a brutal heat season.

drivers.headline: Trump says Israel and Hezbollah agree to halt hostilities; Israel-Lebanon talks open at the US State Department
Tentative US-Iran understanding would extend the ceasefire by 60 days and reopen the Strait of Hormuz
Israeli strikes kill at least eight in Lebanon overnight as Iran briefly suspends talks over the fighting
Ceasefires in Gaza, Lebanon and Iran are stretching the term's meaning as strikes and new violations persist
Sudan's war grinds on as an SAF drone strike on a wedding in Kutum is reported to have killed dozens
Sudan's central bank resumes operations in Khartoum for the first time since the civil war began
Global markets rise as Hang Seng gains about 2.5% and AI enthusiasm holds, though the Nikkei slips 0.3%
Latin America set to grow at twice the pace of the last decade in 2026, led by metals prices and FDI inflows
UN warns the hottest year on record is almost certain by 2030 as Europe's record May heatwave reverberates
Wind and solar generated more of the world's electricity than gas in April, at 22% versus 20%
Platypuses breed along Australia's Hacking River for the first time in generations after a reintroduction
Physicists in China report the first direct observation of the Migdal effect, aiding the search for dark matter
